briefdescription.para: Uses one color from the sequence for the whole bitmap.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is animationCycle + colorAnimationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in vertical stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is x + animationCycle + colorAnimationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in horizontal stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is y + animationCycle + colorAnimationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in forward diagonal stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is x + y + animationCycle + colorAnimationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in backward diagonal stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is -x + y + animationCycle + colorAnimationOffset.

briefdescription.para: The mode how color is applied to the bitmap.
Draw the bitmap with half-blocks.

detaileddescription.para: This mode uses the 16 characters from halfBlocks(). Each 2x2 pixel block creates a 4-bit index in this order: bit 0 = top-left, bit 1 = top-right, bit 2 = bottom-left, bit 3 = bottom-right. This renders the bitmap at half width and half height, rounded up. Character colors are overlaid on the color from the color mode.

briefdescription.para: Draw the bitmap with full-blocks.

detaileddescription.para: This mode uses fullBlock() to draw each set pixel of the bitmap. Character colors are overlaid on the color from the color mode. To color the unset pixels, you must fill the bitmap area first.

briefdescription.para: Draw the bitmap with double-blocks.

detaileddescription.para: This mode uses doubleBlocks() to draw each set pixel of the bitmap. The bitmap is twice as large in the X axis, to compensate for the rectangular shape of terminal characters. Character colors are overlaid on the color from the color mode. To color the unset pixels, you must fill the bitmap area first.

briefdescription.para: The mode how the bitmap is scaled.
Resize using the fastest available path. Existing content order becomes undefined.
Preserve the visible content and fill newly created cells with the fill character.
Describes how a buffer resize should handle existing content.
The type color.
Throw an std::invalid_argument when invalid input is encountered.
Skip unsupported input when the calling API allows lossy recovery.
Replace unsupported input with the Unicode replacement character when supported.
How to handle encoding or Unicode normalization errors.
The top outer line.
The bottom outer line.
The left outer line.
The right outer line.
The horizontal separator lines between rows.
The vertical separator lines between columns.
Named line groups for a grid frame border.
Uses one color from the sequence for the whole frame or fill area.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is animationCycle + animationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in vertical stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is x + animationCycle + animationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in horizontal stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is y + animationCycle + animationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in forward diagonal stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is x + y + animationCycle + animationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence in backward diagonal stripes.

detaileddescription.para: The selected sequence entry is -x + y + animationCycle + animationOffset.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence along the frame border in clockwise order.

detaileddescription.para: Increasing animationCycle makes the colors travel clockwise.

briefdescription.para: Uses the color sequence along the frame border in clockwise order.

detaileddescription.para: Increasing animationCycle makes the colors travel counter-clockwise.

briefdescription.para: The mode how animated colors are applied to frames.
No visible frame; regular frame drawing uses colored spaces.
Light box ┌─┐
Light box with double-dashed lines ┌╌┐
Light box with triple-dashed lines ┌┄┐
Light box with quadruple-dashed lines ┌┈┐
Heavy box ┏━┓
Heavy box with double-dashed lines ┏╍┓
Heavy box with triple-dashed lines ┏┅┓
Heavy box with quadruple-dashed lines ┏┉┓
Double box ╔═╗
Light box with rounded corners ╭─╮
Solid block frame █
Solid block frame with chamfered corners ◢█◣
Half-block frame drawn on the outer cell edges.
Half-block frame drawn on the inner cell edges.
Various box styles.
Top aligned vertically.
Vertically centered.
Bottom aligned vertically.
Left aligned horizontally.
Horizontally centered.
Right aligned horizontally.
Mask for extracting the vertical component.
Mask for extracting the horizontal component.
Anchor describes a location inside a rectangular area using a combination of a vertical and a horizontal component.

listitem.para: Vertical components occupy the lower two bits (VMask).
Horizontal components occupy the next two bits (HMask). Common composite anchors (e.g. TopLeft, Center) are provided for convenience.

briefdescription.para: The Shift key.
The Control key.
The Alt key.
A modifier pressed together with a key.
Absolute move.
Relative move.
The cursor move mode.
Do not extend the wrapped-text background into additional cells.

para.verbatim: No extra cells are painted on the right side of wrapped lines. Continuation indents keep the existing background in buffer-based rendering, or use the current cursor-writer background when the paragraph is printed as streamed output.

briefdescription.para: Extend the wrapped-line background into the left indentation area only.

para.verbatim: The indentation area of each wrapped continuation line uses the background color of the last visible character on the previous physical line. The right side of the line keeps the existing buffer background in buffer-based rendering, or the current cursor-writer background in streamed output. @code | This is a very long | | <fill here> wrapped line. | @endcode

briefdescription.para: Extend the wrapped-line background into the remaining cells on the right side only.

para.verbatim: Each wrapped physical line fills the unused cells up to the available width with the background color of its last visible character. Indentation keeps the existing buffer background in buffer-based rendering, or the current cursor-writer background in streamed output. @code | This is a very long <filled here> | | wrapped line. | @endcode

briefdescription.para: Extend the wrapped-line background on both sides of wrapped lines.
Extend the background into the right-side remainder on every physical line.
Extend the background on both sides for every physical line.
How paragraph rendering extends the background color beyond the visible text.

detaileddescription.para: These modes are most visible when the rendered text uses a non-default background color and the paragraph wraps across multiple physical lines. Depending on the chosen mode, the renderer can extend that background into the unused cells on the right side of a wrapped line, into the indentation area of the continuation line, or both.

For buffer-based text rendering such as Text and WritableBuffer::drawText(), untouched cells keep the existing buffer background. For cursor-writer output such as CursorWriter::printParagraph(), indentation and padding must be materialized as spaces, so cells not covered by wrapped-text background use the writer's current background.

briefdescription.para: Fallback to plain text output and let the terminal handle wrapping.
Do not output the paragraph at all.
The fallback to use when paragraph layout becomes impossible.

detaileddescription.para: This can happen when the available width is too small for the chosen indentation, wrap markers, ellipsis marker, or other paragraph settings.

briefdescription.para: Render the next paragraph directly below the previous paragraph.
Insert one empty row between paragraphs.
The spacing between explicit newline-separated paragraphs.
Replace the tab with a single space character.
End the current physical line and continue after the tab on the next wrapped line.
The handling for tabs whose configured tab stop does not advance the current line.

detaileddescription.para: This mode is used when a left-aligned paragraph encounters a tab stop that is less than or equal to the current column, or when there is no further configured tab stop.

briefdescription.para: Disables signal handling to restore the screen when the application is terminated.

detaileddescription.para: If this flag is set, you must ensure that the restoreScreen() method is called when the application is terminated by a signal. Otherwise, the terminal will not be restored properly.

briefdescription.para: A terminal flag.
No animation.
Diagonal color animation. Requires a color sequence.
Supported text animation styles used by Buffer::renderText().
Shared pointer for Char16Style.
Shared pointer for CharCombinationStyle.
A list of terminal colors.
The background color.
Short alias for Background.
The foreground color.
Short alias for Foreground.
Shared pointer for FastCharSet.
Shared pointer to a terminal font.
A list of input definitions.
A sequence of wrapped terminal text lines.
Shared pointer to a terminal instance.
Shared pointer for Tile9Style.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Anchor lhs, const Anchor rhs) noexcept
memberdef.name: operator|

briefdescription.para: Bitwise OR for combining anchors.

detaileddescription.para: Example: Anchor::Top | Anchor::HCenter -> TopCenter

memberdef.argsstring: (const Anchor lhs, const Anchor rhs) noexcept
memberdef.name: operator&

briefdescription.para: Bitwise AND for masking/extracting components of an anchor.

detaileddescription.para: Example: (anchor & Anchor::VMask) yields the vertical component.

briefdescription.para: Combine two key modifiers into one modifier set.

simplesect.para: A modifier set containing both modifiers.

briefdescription.para: Combine two terminal flags into one flag set.

simplesect.para: A flag set containing both flags.
